artifact.content_preview:
From: noc-team@ssc-spc.gc.ca
To: alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: Network Operations Centre — Monitoring Tooling Question
Date: March 7, 2026

Hi Alice,

During our review of the Site A migration runbook, our NOC team raised
a question that we wanted to flag to the project.

Currently, the NOC uses SolarWinds NPM for network monitoring across all
GC infrastructure. As part of the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment, we
will need to ensure that the new switches are properly onboarded into
the SolarW

extracted_tasks.text: Ensure new Catalyst 9500 switches are onboarded into SolarWinds with appropriate polling intervals and alert thresholds
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: we will need to ensure that the new switches are properly onboarded into the SolarWinds platform

extracted_tasks.text: Upgrade SolarWinds NPM module to version 2024.1 to support Catalyst 9500 platform
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: SolarWinds' support for the Catalyst 9500 platform requires upgrading the NPM module to version 2024.1

extracted_tasks.text: Determine if the NPM upgrade should be included in NGIS Phase 3 network migration activities or handled separately
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Should the NPM upgrade be included as part of the NGIS Phase 3 network migration activities? Or should it be handled separately?

extracted_tasks.text: Classify the work regarding whether it constitutes 'network monitoring tool upgrades' within the project scope
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: We defer to the project team's judgment on how to classify this work

metadata.subject: Network Operations Centre — Monitoring Tooling Question

alerts.detected_ask: Inclusion of the SolarWinds NPM module upgrade (version 2024.1) and associated database schema changes as part of the NGIS Phase 3 network migration activities to support new Catalyst 9500 switches.
alerts.baseline_reference: OUT OF SCOPE: Network monitoring tool upgrades
alerts.suggested_action: Formally reject the inclusion of the SolarWinds NPM upgrade in the current project scope. Classify this as a separate operational change request or a post-project initiative to be funded and executed outside the $2,400,000 budget cap. Initiate a risk assessment for the potential monitoring gap durin

{
  "in_scope": [
    "Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure",
    "Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A",
    "Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A",
    "Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization at Site A",
    "Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites",
    "Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones",
    "BGP routing table migration and validation",
    "Network performance baseline testing post-migration",
    "Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to consolidated infrastructure at Site A",
    "AD schema extension and forest restructuring",
    "Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing",
    "Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD",
    "Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office",
    "Steering committee presentations (monthly)",
    "Risk register maintenance",
    "Change management and stakeholder communications"
  ],
  "out_of_scope": [
    "End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices)",
    "Application modernization or re-platforming (changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform)",
    "GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud)",
    "End-user training and change management beyond project communications",
    "Procurement of new application licenses",
    "Network monitoring tool upgrades"
  ],
  "deliverables": [
    "Project Management Plan",
    "Technical Design Document (TDD)",
    "Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package",
    "Migration Runbook (Sites B and C)",
    "User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan",
    "Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live)",
    "Post-Migration Validation Report",
    "Project Closure Report"
  ],
  "constraints": [
    "Total approved budget cap of $2,400,000 inclusive of all fees and taxes",
    "Budget variance exceeding 15% requires formal amendment and Treasury Board submission",
    "Go-Live deadline no later than April 30, 2026",
    "Final project closure date no later than June 30, 2026",
    "Extensions beyond closure date require ADM-level approval",
    "Compliance with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management",
    "Compliance with Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy",
    "Compliance with Treasury Board Policy on Government Security",
    "Compliance with Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)",
    "Compliance with Official Languages Act for client-facing deliverables",
    "Scope changes require written approval from Project Sponsor and impact assessment"
  ]
}

llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "alignment_score": 0.25,
  "drift_type": "new_requirement",
  "detected_ask": "Inclusion of the SolarWinds NPM module upgrade (version 2024.1) and associated database schema changes as part of the NGIS Phase 3 network migration activities to support new Catalyst 9500 switches.",
  "baseline_reference": "OUT OF SCOPE: Network monitoring tool upgrades",
  "suggested_action": "Formally reject the inclusion of the SolarWinds NPM upgrade in the current project scope. Classify this as a separate operational change request or a post-project initiative to be funded and executed outside the $2,400,000 budget cap. Initiate a risk assessment for the potential monitoring gap during the interim period.",
  "reasoning": "The document explicitly requests an upgrade to the SolarWinds NPM module and database schema changes. The project baseline clearly categorizes 'Network monitoring tool upgrades' as OUT OF SCOPE. While the request is triggered by the in-scope switch deployment, the specific action of upgrading the monitoring software itself constitutes a scope expansion/new requirement that violates the exclusion criteria."
}
```

parsed_json.detected_ask: Inclusion of the SolarWinds NPM module upgrade (version 2024.1) and associated database schema changes as part of the NGIS Phase 3 network migration activities to support new Catalyst 9500 switches.
parsed_json.baseline_reference: OUT OF SCOPE: Network monitoring tool upgrades
parsed_json.suggested_action: Formally reject the inclusion of the SolarWinds NPM upgrade in the current project scope. Classify this as a separate operational change request or a post-project initiative to be funded and executed outside the $2,400,000 budget cap. Initiate a risk assessment for the potential monitoring gap during the interim period.
parsed_json.reasoning: The document explicitly requests an upgrade to the SolarWinds NPM module and database schema changes. The project baseline clearly categorizes 'Network monitoring tool upgrades' as OUT OF SCOPE. While the request is triggered by the in-scope switch deployment, the specific action of upgrading the monitoring software itself constitutes a scope expansion/new requirement that violates the exclusion criteria.
